briefdescription.para: Callback type for all interrupts defined.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XDSIRXSS_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xdsirxss_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XDsi2RxSs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each instance of the device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique address of the device being looked up

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Index number of config table using BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the instance structure
address of the instance

simplesect.para: Index number of the config table

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the MIPI DSI RX subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to configure the DSI2RX default parameters that are to be handled by the application.

detaileddescription.para: It will configure protocol register with Pixel mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful configuration of parameters

briefdescription.para: This function is used to activate the DSI2RX Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it activates the DPHY and DSI2RX. Enable/Disable IP core to start processing

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is used to denote the subcore of subsystem
is used to denote whether to enable or disable the subsystem

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS is returned if subcore(DSI/DPHY) was successfully enabled or disabled XST_INVALID_PARAM is returned if subsystem core is not found

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the DSI2RX Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it resets the DPHY and DSI

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of cores included in DSI RX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI RX Subsystem instance.

briefdescription.para: This function performs self test on MIPI DSI Rx Subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDsi2RxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information from the GUI settings.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information from the GUI settings.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi2RxSs instance to operate on

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get pixel format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DsiRxSs Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0x0E â€“ Packed RGB565 0x1E- packed RGB666 0x2E â€“ Loosely packed RGB666 0x3E- Packed RGB888 0x0B- Compressed Pixel Stream

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XDSI2RX_HANDLER_UNSUPPORT_DATATYPE Unsupported data type XDSI2RX_HANDLER_CRC_ERROR CRC error XDSI2RX_HANDLER_ECC1_BIT_ERROR ECC 1 bit error XDSI2RX_HANDLER_ECC2_BIT_ERROR ECC 2 bit error XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_SYNC_ERR_LANE1 SOT sync error on line 1 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_ERR_LANE1 SOT error on line 1 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_SYNC_ERR_LANE2 SOT sync error on line 2 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_ERR_LANE2 SOT error on line 2 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_SYNC_ERR_LANE3 SOT sync error on line 3 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_ERR_LANE3 SOT error on line 3 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_SYNC_ERR_LANE4 SOT sync error on line 4 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_SOT_ERR_LANE4 SOT error on line 4 XDSI2RX_HANDLER_STOP_STATE STOP state XDSI2RX_HANDLER_LM_ASYNC_FIFO_FULL Long msg asyn fifo full. XDSI2RX_HANDLER_STREAM_ASYNC_FIFO_FULL stream async fifo full XDSI2RX_HANDLER_GSP_FIFO_NE generic short packet fifo not empty XDSI2RX_HANDLER_GSP_FIFO_FULL generic short packet fifo full XDSI2RX_HANDLER_FRAME_STARTED frame started

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi2Rx instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallbackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI DSI2 Rx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XDsi2RxSs_SetCallback() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDsi2RxSs core instance that just interrupted.
